author: Augustine of Hippo

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what way the Church should fear to defile her feet, while proceeding on her way to Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I Have not been unmindful of my debt, and acknowledge that the time of payment has now come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May He give me wherewith to pay, as He gave me cause to incur the debt.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He has given me the love, of which it is said, “Owe no man anything, but to love one another.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May He give also the word, which I feel myself owing to those I love.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I put off your expectations till now for this reason, that I might explain as I could how it is we come to Christ along the ground, when we are commanded rather to seek the things which are above, not the things which are upon the earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ is sitting above, at the right hand of the Father: but He is assuredly here also; and for that reason said also to Saul, as he was raging on the earth, “Why persecutest thou me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the topic on which we were speaking, and which led to our entering on this inquiry, was our Lord’s washing His disciples’ feet, after the disciples themselves had already been washed, and needed not, save to wash their feet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And we there saw it to be understood that a man is indeed wholly washed in baptism; but while thereafter he liveth in this present world, and with the feet of his human passions treadeth on this earth, that is, in his life-intercourse with others, he contracts enough to call forth the prayer, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thus from these also is he cleansed by Him who washed His disciples’ feet, and ceaseth not to make intercession for us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And here occurred the words of the Church in the Song of Songs, when she saith, “I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?” when she wished to go and open to that Being, fairer in form than the sons of men, who had come to her and knocked, and asked her to open to Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This gave rise to a question, which we were unwilling to compress into the narrow limits of the time, and therefore deferred till now, in what sense the Church, when on her way to Christ, may be afraid of defiling her feet, which she had washed in the baptism of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thus she speaks: “I sleep, but my heart waketh: it is the voice of my Beloved that knocketh at the gate.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then He also says: “Open to me, my sister, my nearest, my dove, my perfect one; for my head is filled with dew, and my hair with the drops of the night.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And she replies: “I have put off my dress; how shall I put it on?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O wonderful sacramental symbol!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O lofty mystery!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does she, then, fear to defile her feet in coming to Him who washed the feet of His disciples?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Her fear is genuine; for it is along the earth she has to come to Him, who is still on earth, because refusing to leave His own who are stationed here.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not He that saith, “Lo, I am with you always, even unto the end of the world”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not He that saith, “Ye shall see the heavens opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they ascend to Him because He is above, how do they descend to Him, but because He is also here?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore saith the Church: “I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She says so even in the case of those who, purified from all dross, can say: “I desire to depart, and to be with Christ; nevertheless to abide in the flesh is more needful for you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She says it in those who preach Christ, and open to Him the door, that He may dwell by faith in the hearts of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In such she says it, when they deliberate whether to undertake such a ministry, for which they do not consider themselves qualified, so as to discharge it blamelessly, and so as not, after preaching to others, themselves to become castaways.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is safer to hear than to preach the truth: for in the hearing, humility is preserved; but when it is preached, it is scarcely possible for any man to hinder the entrance of some small measure of boasting, whereby the feet at least are defiled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, as the Apostle James saith, “Let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As it is also said by another man of God, “Thou wilt make me to hear joy and gladness; and the bones Thou hast humbled will rejoice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is what I said: When the truth is heard, humility is preserved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And another says: “But the friend of the bridegroom standeth and heareth him, and rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us rejoice in the hearing that comes from the noiseless speaking of the truth within us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For although, when the sound is outwardly uttered, as by one that readeth; or proclaimeth, or preacheth, or disputeth, or commandeth, or comforteth, or exhorteth, or even by one that sings or accompanies his voice on an instrument, those who do so may fear to defile their feet, when they aim at pleasing men with the secretly active desire of human applause.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet the one who hears such with a willing and pious mind, has no room for self-gratulation in the labors of others; and with no self-inflation, but with the joy of humility, rejoices because of the Master’s words of truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, in those who hear with willingness and humility, and spend a tranquil life in sweet and wholesome studies, the holy Church will take delight, and may say, “I sleep, and my heart waketh.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is this, “I sleep, and my heart waketh,” but just I sit down quietly to listen?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My leisure is not laid out in nourishing slothfulness, but in acquiring wisdom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I sleep, and my heart waketh.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am still, and see that Thou art the Lord: for “the wisdom of the scribe cometh by opportunity of leisure; and he that hath little business shall become wise.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I sleep, and my heart waketh:” I rest from troublesome business, and my mind turns its attention to divine concerns (or communications).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But while the Church finds delightful repose in those who thus sweetly and humbly sit at her feet, here is one who knocks, and says: “What I tell you in darkness, that speak ye in light; and what ye hear in the ear, that preach ye upon the house-tops.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is His voice, then, that knocks at the gate, and says: “Open to me, my sister, my neighbor, my dove, my perfect one; for my head is filled with dew, and my locks with the drops of the night.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if He had said, Thou art at leisure, and the door is closed against me: thou art caring for the leisure of the few, and through abounding iniquity the love of many is waxing cold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The night He speaks of is iniquity: but His dew and drops are those who wax cold and fall away, and make the head of Christ to wax cold, that is, the love of God to fail.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the head of Christ is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they are borne on His locks, that is, their presence is tolerated in the visible sacraments; while their senses never take hold of the internal realities.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He knocks, therefore, to shake off this quiet from His inactive saints, and cries, “Open to me,” thou who, through my blood, art become “my sister;” through my drawing nigh, “my neighbor;” through my Spirit, “my dove;” through my word which thou hast fully learned in thy leisure, “my perfect one:” open to me, go and preach me to others.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how shall I get in to those who have shut their door against me, without some one to open? and how shall they hear without a preacher?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence it happens that those who love to devote their leisure to good studies, and shrink from encountering the troubles of toilsome labors, as feeling themselves unsuited to undertake and discharge such services with credit, would prefer, were it possible, to have the holy apostles and ancient preachers of the truth again raised up against that abounding of iniquity which hath so reduced the warmth of Christian love.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in regard to those who have already left the body, and put off the garment of the flesh (for they are not utterly parted), the Church replies, “I have put off my dress; how shall I put it on?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That dress shall, indeed, yet be recovered; and in the persons of those who have meanwhile laid it aside, shall the Church again put on the garment of flesh: only not now, when the cold are needing to be warmed; but then, when the dead shall rise again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Realizing, then, her present difficulty through the scarcity of preachers, and remembering those members of her own who were so sound in word and holy in character, but are now disunited from their bodies, the Church says in her sorrow, “I have put off my dress; how shall I put it on?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How can those members of mine, who had such surpassing power, through their preaching, to open the door to Christ, now return to the bodies which they have laid aside?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then, turning again to those who preach, and gather in and govern the congregations of His people, and so open as they can to Christ, but are afraid, amid the difficulties of such work, of falling into sin, she says, “I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For whosoever offendeth not in word, the same is a perfect man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who, then, is perfect?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is there that offendeth not amid such an abounding of iniquity, and such a freezing of charity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At times I read and hear: “My brethren, be not many masters, seeing that ye shall receive the greater condemnation: for in many things we offend all.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But see, I rise and open.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ, wash them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Forgive us our debts,” because our love is not altogether extinguished: for “we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When we listen to Thee, the bones which have been humbled rejoice with Thee in the heavenly places.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when we preach Thee, we have to tread the ground in order to open to Thee: and then, if we are blameworthy, we are troubled; if we are commended, we become inflated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wash our feet, that were formerly cleansed, but have again been defiled in our walking through the earth to open unto Thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let this be enough today, beloved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in whatever we have happened to offend, by saying otherwise than we ought, or have been unduly elated by your commendations, entreat that our feet may be washed, and may your prayers find acceptance with God.
